title: Stakeholder Conflict
description: Sara wants a full viral referral loop — social sharing, tracking links, leaderboard. Rayan says that's 3 sprints minimum. The student must mediate, define a scoped MVP version, and get both to agree in writing before updating the PRD.
context: The student made a sprint decision in scene_2. Sara accepted it reluctantly. Now she has sent Rayan her full feature brief for the referral feature — and Rayan has come back saying it's a 3-sprint build. Both of them are now waiting for the PM to define scope. The conflict is not hostile — it's a genuine misalignment on what 'referral feature' means.

scene_setup.sara_position: Wants the full viral loop: social sharing buttons, unique tracking links per user, a referral leaderboard showing top inviters, and an automated reward trigger when a referral converts. She believes this is what will actually move the Q3 growth metric.
scene_setup.rayan_position: Will build the referral feature but needs it scoped down to something his team can ship in 2 weeks. His estimate: referral link generation only is 1 week. Adding tracking is another week. Social sharing and leaderboard are 4+ additional weeks.
scene_setup.prd_state: PRD from scene_1 is still mostly blank. Success metrics and scope fields are empty. Student must fill them in before the scene can complete.

prd_document.title: Referral Feature — Scoped Brief

sections.overview: Referral feature allowing users to invite friends. Scope TBD.
sections.problem_statement: User growth is below target. Referral programs have shown 30% CAC reduction in comparable products.

prd_document.notes: Student must fill in at least scope, out_of_scope, and success_metrics to complete the scene. The specific content of those fields feeds into branch point scoring.

bp_4.description: How does the student respond to the Sara vs Rayan conflict?

ambiguity_tolerance.high: Asks Sara what the minimum viable version of the referral feature is that would still hit the Q3 OKR, rather than choosing sides or deferring
ambiguity_tolerance.medium: Acknowledges both positions and proposes a general compromise without specifics
ambiguity_tolerance.low: Takes one side completely, avoids the conflict, or says 'let's discuss later'

analytical_reasoning.high: References the Q3 OKR to anchor the scope decision — asks what metric needs to move and what the minimum feature to move it is
analytical_reasoning.medium: Reasons about scope based on engineering capacity without connecting back to the business goal
analytical_reasoning.low: Makes a scope decision with no stated reasoning

stakeholder_management.high: Addresses both Sara and Rayan in the same message or back-to-back — doesn't leave one waiting while solving the other's problem
stakeholder_management.medium: Addresses one stakeholder and follows up with the other shortly after
stakeholder_management.low: Only addresses one stakeholder or sends a message to neither

communication_clarity.high: Proposes a specific reduced scope, not a vague 'compromise' — e.g. 'referral link generation in v1, leaderboard in v2'
communication_clarity.medium: Proposes a compromise but without specifics
communication_clarity.low: Gives a vague response that doesn't resolve the conflict

good_signals: asks Sara what minimum viable version hits the Q3 OKR
asks Rayan what's realistic in 2 weeks
proposes a specific reduced scope (not just 'let's compromise')
references the Q3 OKR to anchor the decision
proposes leaderboard and social sharing as v2 roadmap items
bad_signals: takes Sara's side and tells Rayan to make it work
takes Rayan's side without explaining the trade-off to Sara
says 'let's get everyone on a call' without making a decision
gives a vague 'we'll find a solution' response

bp_5.description: Student writes and sends the scope decision to both stakeholders and updates the PRD

decisiveness.high: Sends a clear, specific scope decision without being asked twice
decisiveness.medium: Sends a scope decision after one prompt from Rayan or Sara
decisiveness.low: Still hasn't sent a written scope decision after two prompts

analytical_reasoning.high: Scope decision explains what's included in v1, what's deferred to v2, and why the v1 scope is sufficient to hit the Q3 OKR
analytical_reasoning.medium: Scope decision lists what's in and what's out but doesn't explain why
analytical_reasoning.low: Scope decision is vague or contradicts the earlier mediation response

communication_clarity.high: Message is specific enough that both Sara and Rayan can act on it immediately with no follow-up questions needed
communication_clarity.medium: Message is clear but missing one key piece — e.g. has scope but no timeline, or has timeline but no success metric
communication_clarity.low: Message is vague, too long, or doesn't address both stakeholders

attention_to_detail.high: PRD is updated with scope, out_of_scope, and a success metric before the scene ends
attention_to_detail.medium: PRD is partially updated — one or two fields filled in

good_signals: includes a specific feature definition in the scope message (e.g. 'referral link generation only, no leaderboard, no social sharing in v1')
includes a success metric (e.g. '200 referral link clicks in first 2 weeks')
explicitly states what is out of scope for v1
mentions v2 for the deferred features
sends the message to both Sara and Rayan
updates the PRD scope, out_of_scope, and success_metrics fields
bad_signals: scope message says 'a simple referral feature' without specifics
no success metric in the message or PRD
PRD is not updated at all
scope contradicts what was agreed in mediation

easy.hint_text: Tip: Ask Sara what the minimum version of this feature is that would still hit the Q3 growth target. Then ask Rayan what's realistic in 2 weeks. Use both answers to define the scope.

medium.sara_pushback_message: I appreciate you trying to scope this down but I'm worried a referral link with no leaderboard won't actually drive the behaviour we need. Can we at least keep the leaderboard?

ceo_email.subject: Referral Feature — Status?
ceo_email.body: Sara mentioned there's some back and forth on the referral feature scope. Can you send me a quick update on where we landed and what's shipping this sprint? I have a board call Friday.

hard.sara_pushback_message: Honestly I'm not sure a referral link alone will move the needle. I told the CEO we'd have something more impactful. Can we at least commit to tracking links so we can measure conversion?
hard.rayan_hard_message: If we're adding tracking links on top of referral link generation, that's the full 2 weeks of sprint capacity right there. There's no room for anything else. I need that in writing before I commit.
